text: 6. **Never say Nostr, crypto, or decentralized above the architecture section.** Above the fold the mechanism is described by its properties: "self-owned keys on your disk, no central server to run or trust, works even if a relay dies." The word "Nostr" appears exactly once, in "How it works," framed as: open protocol, self-hostable, no vendor lock-in. [...] 3. **"Nostr / crypto / decentralized" never appears above the architecture section** (crypto dev mindshare is collapsing and "Nostr + AI agent" is already colonized by token plays). Above the fold it's described by properties — *keys on your disk, no central server, works if a relay dies*. Nostr is revealed once, as a credibility signal in "How it works." [...] **Beat 5 — How it works.** Hooks ("the plugin is just the straw — tenex-edge knows nothing about the host"), the daemon, SQLite, fail-open design, the corruption war story in one sentence. **"Built on Nostr" is revealed here and only here**, as three property bullets, then the protocol name. The single permitted use of "citizenship for your agents" closes this section.
